shortAnswer: Ferritin shows how much iron is stored in the body. A low value indicates depleted iron stores, often long before anemia develops. Elevated values may indicate inflammation or iron overload. Discuss any abnormal values with your doctor.
metaDescription: Ferritin low or high? Normal ranges, iron deficiency diagnosis, and causes — explained simply.
doctorQuestions: Do I have iron deficiency even if my hemoglobin is normal?
What is causing my elevated ferritin?
pageTitle: Ferritin value — Iron stores explained | Medyra
